exemplar.title: Investigating the effects of shallow and deep processing through note-taking methods, on the performance of memory recall

detailedExemplarData.title: Investigating the effects of shallow and deep processing through note-taking methods, on the performance of memory recall

subject.title: Psychology
subject.description:
IB Psychology is a Group 3 subject in the International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP). The course examines human behavior and mental processes through scientific inquiry, exploring biological, cognitive, and sociocultural approaches to understanding behavior. Students develop critical thinking skills while investigating contemporary psychological research, research methods, and ethical considerations in psychological practice.

The course is available at Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours) and Higher Level (HL, 240 hours), with first assessment in 2027.

Assessment consists of Paper 1 (integration of concepts, content, and contexts through short-answer and extended-response questions: SL 2 hours, HL 2 hours, SL 50%, HL 40%), Paper 2 (application of psychological knowledge to research contexts: SL 1 hour, HL 2 hours, SL 25%, HL 20%), Paper 3 (HL only: research methods and data analysis, 1 hour, 20%), and Internal Assessment (research proposal development: SL 25%, HL 20%).

strengths: Clear, concise aim with educational and theoretical relevance explained
Accurate description of Levels of Processing theory with semantic vs structural/phonemic distinction
Explicit identification and full operationalization of IV and DV in hypotheses
weaknesses: Relevance explanation remains general without specific empirical or societal statistics
Connection between theory and chosen note-taking methods could be more explicitly articulated
Hypotheses wording could reference the operationalized DV (âscore out of 18â) more precisely
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The introduction strands received two band-5 assessments and one band-6 assessment. The aim and theory are clearly explained, and the variables are fully operationalized. Minor omissions in empirical grounding and explicit theoretical linkage place it solidly in the 5-6 band but not at the very top, warranting a score of 5.

strengths: Research design choice (independent measures) is justified in terms of construct validity and demand characteristics
Sampling technique and participant characteristics are linked to control of prior knowledge and gender balance
Controlled variables (same video, quiz, timing) are well identified
weaknesses: No rationale for omitting counterbalancing or addressing order effects
Environmental controls (e.g., room conditions) are not discussed
Justification for material choice (e.g., TED Talk selection) lacks direct link to cognitive processes
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Two strands were placed in the 3-4 band and one in the 1-2 band. Although design and sampling are well explained, the partial justification of materials and missing procedural details limit the work to the mid-band, resulting in a score of 3 out of 4.

strengths: Appropriate selection and execution of a non-parametric Mann-Whitney U test with correct p-value interpretation
Graphical technique directly addresses the hypothesis with clear labels and data representation
Statistical findings correctly linked to hypothesis direction
weaknesses: Graphical presentation lacks error bars and APA-style captions
No measures of variability (e.g., standard deviations) reduce insight into data dispersion
Effect size is not reported, limiting understanding of practical significance
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three strands sit in the top band (5-6), but consistent omissions in variability measures, error bars, and effect-size reporting prevent a fully accurate application. Thus the analysis is strong but incomplete, meriting a score of 4.

strengths: Findings are discussed with reference to Levels of Processing theory
Strengths and limitations are explicitly tied to validity and design concerns
Modifications are justified and linked to identified limitations
weaknesses: Overinterpretation of a non-significant result risks conflating statistical nonsignificance with theoretical support
Discussion of ecological or mundane realism lacks specifics about lecture context differences
Conclusion could better acknowledge the non-significant outcome for balance
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Evaluation strands are all in the 5-6 band, demonstrating mature critique and justified modifications. However, overinterpretation and missing contextual detail temper the discussionâs completeness. A score of 4 reflects strong evaluation with room for deeper balance and specificity.

strandSelections.reasoning: A bar chart with labelled axes, title and numerical annotations directly addresses the hypothesis and accurately displays means. Minor stylistic issues (e.g. limited error bars) prevent a full 6, but the graphical presentation clearly meets the 5-6 descriptor and earns 5.

The student **links statistical outcome to both data pattern and hypothesis**, correctly concluding that the null is retained. Interpretation references depth-of-processing theory later in Evaluation rather than here, so depth is solid but not exceptional, justifying a score of 5.

Both IV conditions (laptop vs longhand) and the DV (score out of 18) are **explicitly identified and fully operationalised**; the hypotheses are correctly worded as null and directional research statements. Because the operationalisation is precise and measurable, the strand meets the top of the highest band.

Sampling technique (opportunity) and participant characteristics are not only listed but **linked to practicality and control of prior knowledge/gender balance**, demonstrating explanation. The reasoning is adequate though not exhaustive (e.g. no reflection on school context constraints), meriting the top of the 3-4 band.

Levels of Processing theory is **accurately described (L1 vs L2; semantic vs structural/phonemic)** and the link to note-taking depth is clearly articulated. The explanation is coherent but not exceptionally detailed (e.g. lacks reference to empirical parameters such as typical depth-of-processing effect sizes), therefore a 5 rather than 6 represents the quality shown.

Findings are **discussed in relation to Levels of Processing**, articulating why longhand promotes deeper encoding. The discussion integrates descriptive and theoretical aspects but lacks engagement with contrary evidence or effect size, positioning it at 5 within the highest band.

The student states a clear, concise aim â to compare laptop and long-hand note-taking â and then **explains why this is educationally and theoretically relevant**, referring to implications for pedagogy, cognitive psychology and technology use. While the explanation is sound, it remains fairly general and could be strengthened with specific empirical or societal statistics, so a mid-top score of the 5-6 band is warranted.

Multiple strengths (control of IV, avoidance of order effects) and limitations (participant variability, sampling bias, ecological validity) are **explained and explicitly tied to how they may influence validity**, satisfying the highest descriptor though not exhaustively, so 5 is appropriate.

Controlled variables (same video, quiz, timings) and material choice are described and partially justified (video length, factual + conceptual balance). The explanation is present but somewhat thin (e.g. no detail on standardisation of environment), so a mid-band score of 3 is most fitting.

Each limitation identified is paired with a **specific, justified modification** (e.g. screening for cognitive ability, adopting stratified sampling, longitudinal design for ecological validity). The links are clear and rationale provided, albeit briefly, warranting a 5 in the top band.

The student moves beyond description by **justifying the choice of independent-measures design in terms of construct validity and demand characteristics**, satisfying the âexplainedâ descriptor. Minor omissions (e.g. absence of explicit counterbalancing rationale) keep it at the lower end of the 3-4 band, so 4 is awarded.

Mean scores are computed correctly and an **appropriate non-parametric Mann-Whitney U test** is selected, justified, and executed with correct p-value interpretation. No calculation errors are evident; however, effect size is not reported, so the strand sits comfortably at 5 within the top band.

annotations.comment: The student accurately describes Levels of Processing theory, outlining shallow vs deep encoding processes with structural, phonemic, and semantic distinctions. This clarity demonstrates strong grasp of theoretical foundations.

While the theory is well outlined, the connection between Levels of Processing and the chosen note-taking methods is not yet articulated. The student should explicitly link how pen-and-paper vs laptop aligns with deep vs shallow processing.

To deepen the introduction, the student could incorporate empirical effect sizes or key studies quantifying Levels of Processing impacts, which would strengthen theoretical justification for investigating note-taking methods.
The alternative hypothesis could more directly reference the operationalized DV to avoid ambiguity, for instance specifying 'score out of 18 points' in its wording.
The justification for using an independent-measures design would be more robust if it also addressed potential order effects and explained why counterbalancing was deemed unnecessary.
The identification of sampling bias as a limitation, paired with a justified stratified sampling modification, displays mature evaluation and relevance to generalizability.
The limitation regarding mundane realism is noted, but the student could strengthen it by explaining how the TED Talk context diverges from typical classroom lectures.
The investigation aim is clear, but could be strengthened by citing specific statistics or studies on technology use in classrooms to ground its educational relevance in concrete empirical data.
The independent variable is clearly defined with two discrete note-taking conditions (laptop vs longhand), providing a solid operational framework for the investigation.
The dependent variable is precisely operationalized as the number of correct responses out of 18 on a standardized test, ensuring clarity in measurement criteria.
Formatting hypotheses in LaTeX (e.g., $$H_0$$:â¦) would improve clarity. Additionally, explicitly restating the DV as 'score out of 18' in $$H_0$$ would enhance precision.
The sampling method description links participant selection to control of prior knowledge and gender balance, demonstrating thoughtful consideration of sample characteristics.
The procedural description omits discussion of environmental controls (e.g., noise level, seating arrangement), which are important for maintaining consistency across conditions.
The controlled variablesâincluding the same lecture, quiz content, and timingâare well identified, reinforcing the studyâs internal validity.
Incorporating a pilot study demonstrates proactive effort to refine procedure and preempt potential methodological flaws before data collection.
The pilot sample size of two may be insufficient to detect procedural issues. Consider expanding pilot participants to capture a wider range of practical challenges.
The rationale for selecting this specific TED Talk could be elaborated by linking its factual and conceptual content directly to the cognitive processes under investigation.
The analysis correctly compares mean scores between conditions and interprets the direction of the difference in relation to the hypothesis.
Reporting only means without measures of variability (e.g., standard deviation or standard error) limits insight into data dispersion and reliability of the central tendency.
Enhance the graphical presentation by adding titles and captions following APA style, explicitly labeling Table 1 and describing its content within the caption.
The bar chart omitted error bars, which are essential to depict variability and allow visual evaluation of statistical significance.
Present inferential test results in LaTeX, e.g., $$U = 29.5,\ p = 0.0655$$ and include an effect size (e.g., $$r$$) to contextualize the magnitude of the non-significant finding.
The evaluation adeptly integrates the Levels of Processing framework to explain why longhand note-taking may foster deeper encoding and improved recall.
The discussion interprets a non-significant result as evidence of deep processing benefits, conflating statistical nonsignificance with theoretical support and risking overinterpretation.
Several strengths and limitations are clearly linked to the independent-measures design, demonstrating awareness of design-specific validity concerns.
Proposing a longitudinal design to allow participants to study notes over time directly addresses the ecological validity concern raised earlier.
Conclude by briefly summarizing how the findings align (or not) with the hypothesis, and acknowledge the non-significant outcome to ensure a balanced conclusion.
The reference list shows inconsistencies in citation formatting and retrieval date inclusion; standardize entries to adhere fully to APA or chosen style guidelines.
